21.description: File Transfer Protocol transmits data in plaintext. Credentials and files can be intercepted.
21.recommendation: Disable FTP. Use SFTP (port 22) or FTPS instead.

22.description: Secure Shell is encrypted. Exposure is acceptable but should be hardened.
22.recommendation: Disable root login, use key-based auth, and restrict by IP if possible.

23.description: Telnet sends ALL data including passwords in plaintext. Considered obsolete.
23.recommendation: Disable Telnet immediately. Replace with SSH.

25.description: Mail server open to the internet may be abused for spam relaying.
25.recommendation: Restrict to authenticated users only. Enable STARTTLS.

53.description: Open DNS can be used for amplification attacks or zone transfer abuse.
53.recommendation: Restrict zone transfers. Consider DNS over HTTPS (DoH).

80.description: Unencrypted web traffic. Credentials and session data transmitted in plaintext.
80.recommendation: Force redirect to HTTPS (port 443). Use HSTS headers.

110.description: Email retrieval in plaintext. Credentials easily captured on the network.
110.recommendation: Disable POP3. Use POP3S (port 995) or IMAP over TLS.

135.description: Microsoft RPC endpoint mapper. Common attack vector for Windows exploits.
135.recommendation: Block port 135 on the firewall perimeter. Restrict to internal network only.

137.description: NetBIOS Name Service leaks internal hostnames and network topology.
137.recommendation: Disable NetBIOS. Block ports 137-139 at the firewall.

139.description: NetBIOS Session Service. Used in legacy SMB. Exploited in EternalBlue (WannaCry).
139.recommendation: Disable NetBIOS over TCP/IP. Use SMB2/3 over port 445 only.

143.description: Email access in plaintext. Password sniffing risk on the network.

443.description: Encrypted web traffic. Standard secure web server port.
443.recommendation: Verify TLS version (TLS 1.2+ required). Check certificate validity.

445.description: Server Message Block. Exploited by EternalBlue (WannaCry, NotPetya). High-risk exposure.
445.recommendation: Block SMB from the internet immediately. Apply MS17-010 patch. Use SMB signing.

512.description: Remote Execution service. Legacy Unix protocol transmitting credentials in plaintext.
512.recommendation: Disable rexec, rlogin, rsh services. Use SSH exclusively.

513.description: Remote Login — legacy Unix protocol with no encryption.

514.description: Remote Shell or Syslog. RSH has no authentication. Syslog logs can be intercepted.
514.recommendation: Disable RSH. For Syslog, use TLS-encrypted syslog (RFC 5425).

1433.description: Microsoft SQL Server exposed to the network. Direct database access risk.
1433.recommendation: Never expose database ports to the internet. Restrict to application server IPs only.

1521.description: Oracle Database listener exposed. Direct database access risk.
1521.recommendation: Block at firewall. Restrict to application tier only.

2049.description: Network File System with no built-in encryption. File access without credentials possible.
2049.recommendation: Restrict NFS exports to specific IPs. Use NFSv4 with Kerberos.

3306.description: MySQL database port exposed. Brute-force and data exfiltration risk.
3306.recommendation: Bind MySQL to 127.0.0.1 only. Never expose to the internet.

3389.description: Remote Desktop Protocol. High-value target — BlueKeep, DejaBlue exploits. Brute force risk.
3389.recommendation: Disable if unused. Use VPN + RDP. Enable NLA. Restrict by IP.

4444.description: Default Metasploit reverse shell port. Active attack or malware indicator.
4444.recommendation: Investigate immediately. Block and audit all connections to this port.

5432.description: PostgreSQL database exposed to network. Unauthorized access risk.
5432.recommendation: Restrict to localhost or application server. Never expose publicly.

5900.description: VNC remote desktop — often misconfigured with weak or no passwords.
5900.recommendation: Disable VNC. If needed, tunnel over SSH. Enforce strong passwords.

6379.description: Redis cache server. Default config has NO authentication. Widely exploited.
6379.recommendation: Bind to 127.0.0.1. Set requirepass. Never expose Redis to the internet.

8080.description: Alternative HTTP port. Often used by dev/test servers or proxy servers.
8080.recommendation: Ensure dev/test servers are not publicly accessible. Use HTTPS.

8443.description: Alternative HTTPS port. Verify certificate and TLS configuration.
8443.recommendation: Ensure valid TLS certificate and up-to-date TLS version.

9200.description: Elasticsearch exposed with NO authentication by default. Millions of records leaked globally.
9200.recommendation: Add authentication immediately. Bind to localhost. Never expose publicly.

27017.description: MongoDB with no authentication by default. Has caused major data breaches.
27017.recommendation: Enable authentication. Bind to 127.0.0.1. Restrict at firewall.
